keyPointsSummary: Use 'There is' with uncountable nouns (water, milk, rice, money)
The real subject comes after 'is', not before it
Never say 'There is many' — that is WRONG
Correct: There is a book. Wrong: There is books.
Use 'There are' with numbers more than one (two, three, many, several)
Correct: There are many students. Wrong: There is many students.
There are + a few = थोड़े हैं
There are + a lot of = बहुत सारे हैं
Use with yesterday, last week, last year, in 2020, etc.
There was a time... = एक समय था...

quickQuiz.answer: There is a book on the table.

quickQuiz.hindi: मेरे कमरे में एक पंखा है।
quickQuiz.answer: There is a fan in my room.
There is a problem in the system.
There is a meeting at 3 pm.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमारी कक्षा में एक नया छात्र है।
quickQuiz.answer: There is a new student in our class.

quickQuiz.hindi: मेरे घर के पास एक मंदिर है।
quickQuiz.answer: There is a temple near my house.
There is some water in the glass.
There is a lot of work today.

quickQuiz.hindi: दरवाजे के बाहर एक कुत्ता है।
quickQuiz.answer: There is a dog outside the door.

quickQuiz.hindi: यहाँ के पास एक बस स्टॉप है।
quickQuiz.answer: There is a bus stop near here.

mustRememberRules: Use 'There is' with uncountable nouns (water, milk, rice, money)
The real subject comes after 'is', not before it
Never say 'There is many' — that is WRONG
Correct: There is a book. Wrong: There is books.
